---
title: "Where OrgTree & OrgPath Data Lives ‚Äî Devices and Azure Resources"
subtitle: "Two Storage Slots, One Codebook, Disposition-Driven Routing"
date: 2026-05-11
---

::: {.callout-note}
## Companion to the user-side explainer

This page picks up from [Where OrgTree & OrgPath Data Lives](where-data-lives.qmd),
which covered the user case (HR ‚Üí `user.extensionAttribute1` ‚Üê
codebook). Computers and Azure resources decompose across more planes
than users do, so the storage story extends slightly ‚Äî but the
codebook and authority layers are unchanged. Read the user-side page
first.
:::

[IMAGE-REF: UIAO-FIG-014]

## Why this needs its own page

A user has exactly one OrgPath storage slot: `user.extensionAttribute1`.
A computer doesn't. Per [ADR-034](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-034-three-plane-device-model.md),
a computer object decomposes across three planes:

| Plane | Object type | OrgPath storage | Targeting |
|-------|-------------|-----------------|-----------|
| **Identity** (clients) | Entra Device | `extensionAttribute1` | Dynamic Entra group |
| **Management** (clients) | Intune enrollment | *Inherited* from Entra device | Intune scope tag |
| **Management** (servers) | Azure Arc machine | ARM tag `OrgPath` | Azure Policy assignment |
| **Workload** | Service Principal / Managed Identity | `servicePrincipal.extensionAttribute1` (per ADR-063) | App role / API permission |

The two storage slots that actually carry an OrgPath string are
`extensionAttribute1` (Entra) and the ARM tag `OrgPath` (Azure
Resource Manager). The other two rows *inherit* from those ‚Äî Intune
reads from the Entra device, workload-identity governance derives
from the app registration's tagging or role assignment.

## The two device storage slots

These two writes are governed by
[`src/uiao/canon/data/orgpath/device-planes.yaml`](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/data/orgpath/device-planes.yaml),
the executable canon for which plane gets written for which
disposition. The dispatch happens inside the
`entra-device-orgpath` adapter ‚Äî Graph/ARM endpoint strings live in
canon, not in code (ADR-038).

### Slot 1: `extensionAttribute1` on the Entra device

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Transport | Microsoft Graph |
| Target | `directoryObject/device` |
| Method | `PATCH /devices/{object_id}` |
| Body | `onPremisesExtensionAttributes.extensionAttribute1: "<OrgPath>"` |
| Used when disposition is | `ENTRA-DEVICE` |

This is the client side ‚Äî workstations, laptops, Entra-joined or
hybrid-joined endpoints. The same surface Entra Connect syncs into
for user `extensionAttribute1` (ADR-035 Phase 1).

### Slot 2: ARM tag `OrgPath` on the Azure Arc machine

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Transport | Azure Resource Manager |
| Target | `Microsoft.HybridCompute/machines` |
| Method | `PATCH {arm_resource_id}?api-version=2023-03-15-preview` |
| Body | `tags.OrgPath: "<OrgPath>"` |
| Used when disposition is | `ARC-SERVER`, `STAY-AD-DEPENDENCY`, `MANAGED-IDENTITY-CANDIDATE` |

This is the server side ‚Äî anything Arc-enrolled, including
Arc-visible AD-dependency boxes and workload-identity-candidate
servers during the migration window.

### Skipped slots

| Disposition | Why no write |
|-------------|--------------|
| `STAY-AD-DC` | Domain controllers don't migrate; OrgPath is not written |
| `DECOMMISSION` | End-of-life; no write attempted |

## The agreement rule

Both slots validate against the *exact same* canon:

- **Same regex:** `^ORG(-[A-Z0-9]{2,6}){0,8}$`
- **Same codebook:** [`codebook.yaml`](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/data/orgpath/codebook.yaml)
- **Same authority:** the codebook entry's parent of record. For
  workstations this is the assigned user's HR record; for servers it's
  the workload registration in the disposition matrix.
- **Same drift semantics:** Schema, Value, Hierarchy, Orphan, and
  Phantom ‚Äî see [Drift Detection Walk-through](drift-walkthrough.qmd).
  Devices do not get their own drift categories.

A device that is *both* Entra-joined and Arc-enrolled (rare but
possible, e.g., dev VMs) is canonically required to carry the same
OrgPath in both slots ‚Äî the two slots validate against the same
codebook and the canon treats string equality between them as a
required invariant.

::: {.callout-warning title="Runtime enforcement of cross-plane equality is deferred"}
As of UIAO_163 v1, the drift engine reads the Graph plane
(`extensionAttribute1`) but does *not* perform a cross-plane equality
check against the ARM `OrgPath` tag. Today's enforcement is
**adapter-side** in `EntraDeviceOrgPathAdapter`
([`src/uiao/adapters/entra_device_orgpath.py`](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/adapters/entra_device_orgpath.py))
which dispatches the write to the correct transport for the
disposition. A future ADR (companion to the `DRIFT-SCHEMA::slot-occupied`
sub-class deferred in ADR-063) will introduce the cross-plane equality
check; until then, dual-plane disagreement is detected on the next
codebook-driven re-write rather than as a runtime drift event.
:::

## What is *not* in this story

- **Intune scope tags** derive from the Entra device's
  `extensionAttribute1`. The scope-tag value is a function of the
  OrgPath, not an independent storage location. See
  [Customer narrative ch. 5 ‚Äî OrgPath and Intune](../customer-documents/orgpath-narrative/05-orgpath-and-intune.qmd)
  for the device-lifecycle treatment.
- **Azure Policy `OrgPath` enforcement on non-Arc resources** uses
  the ARM tag the same way, but the tag is written by template
  defaults or `Modify` policies, not by the `entra-device-orgpath`
  adapter. See
  [Customer narrative ch. 9 ‚Äî OrgPath and Azure Policy / Guest
  Config](../customer-documents/orgpath-narrative/09-orgpath-and-azure-policy-guest-config.qmd)
  and
  [ch. 12 ‚Äî OrgPath in Infrastructure Services](../customer-documents/orgpath-narrative/12-orgpath-in-infrastructure-services.qmd)
  for the Azure resource tagging pattern (private DNS zones, key
  vaults, storage accounts, etc.).
- **Service Principal / Managed Identity OrgPath** is canonically
  in-scope (ADR-063) but currently consumed only by app-registration
  reviews; the runtime drift engine reads the user and device planes
  only.

## Quick reference

| Question | Answer |
|----------|--------|
| Where is a workstation's OrgPath? | `device.extensionAttribute1` in Entra |
| Where is an Arc server's OrgPath? | ARM tag `OrgPath` on the `Microsoft.HybridCompute/machines` resource |
| Where is an Azure VM's OrgPath? | ARM tag `OrgPath` on the resource; *plus* `device.extensionAttribute1` if the VM is also Entra-joined |
| Where is a domain controller's OrgPath? | Not written. `STAY-AD-DC` is a defined skip disposition in [`device-planes.yaml`](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/data/orgpath/device-planes.yaml) ‚Äî the canon's rule for DCs is explicitly "no write". |
| Where is a Managed Identity's OrgPath? | `servicePrincipal.extensionAttribute1` (per ADR-063); declared in-scope but not consumed by drift v1 |
| What enforces the binding? | `entra-device-orgpath` adapter + `device-planes.yaml` + drift engine (UIAO_163) |
| Who decides which slot? | `ComputerDisposition.orgpath_plane`, computed from the disposition matrix |

## Related

- [Where OrgTree & OrgPath Data Lives](where-data-lives.qmd) ‚Äî the
  user-side story this page extends
- [Drift Detection Walk-through](drift-walkthrough.qmd) ‚Äî the
  shared drift semantics
- [OrgPath Codebook (UIAO_151)](codebook.qmd) ‚Äî the canonical
  enumeration both slots validate against
- [ADR-034 Three-Plane Device Model](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-034-three-plane-device-model.md) ‚Äî
  the architectural decomposition
- [ADR-038 Device-Plane OrgPath](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-038-device-plane-orgpath.md) ‚Äî
  the dual-transport adapter
- [ADR-063 extensionAttribute1 Storage Slot Binding](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-063-orgpath-storage-slot-binding.md) ‚Äî
  the slot ratification covering users, devices, SPs, and ARM tags
- [Customer narrative ch. 5 ‚Äî OrgPath and Intune](../customer-documents/orgpath-narrative/05-orgpath-and-intune.qmd)
- [Customer narrative ch. 9 ‚Äî OrgPath and Azure Policy / Guest Config](../customer-documents/orgpath-narrative/09-orgpath-and-azure-policy-guest-config.qmd)
- [Customer narrative ch. 12 ‚Äî OrgPath in Infrastructure Services](../customer-documents/orgpath-narrative/12-orgpath-in-infrastructure-services.qmd)
